title: FPGA Boards Catalog
description: Schemas for entities (standalone boards, SoMs, carriers, kits, FMC cards) and their relationships. Each entity type has its own folder under board-repo (boards/, soms/, carriers/, kits/, fmc-cards/); relationships live under relationships/<type>/. The build script validates every file in those folders against the matching $def.

standalone.title: Standalone Board
standalone.description: A complete FPGA development board with on-board user I/O — not a SoM, not part of a kit.

memory.title: Memory
memory.description: On-board DRAM. List one entry per distinct memory bank.

flash.title: Flash
flash.description: On-board non-volatile flash memory. List one entry per distinct chip.

eeprom.title: EEPROM
eeprom.description: Small on-board EEPROMs (MAC address, board UID, board identification, user data). One entry per chip.

analog.title: Analog
analog.description: Discrete ADCs, DACs, RF transceivers, and AFEs soldered to the board. Excludes FPGA-internal XADC/SYSMON and anything on daughter cards.

sensors.title: Sensors
sensors.description: On-board sensor chips (IMU, temperature, light, etc.).

pcie.title: PCIe
pcie.description: PCIe interfaces on the board (Edge connector, slot, PCIe/104, iPass, MCIO, MiniPCIe). M.2 sockets and M.2 form-factor boards live in the separate `m2` array.

m2.title: M.2
m2.description: M.2 sockets the board provides, or the board's own M.2 edge connector if it is itself an M.2 module.

usb.title: USB
usb.description: USB interfaces accessible to the FPGA design.

som.title: System-on-Module
som.description: A System-on-Module (SoM) — an FPGA + memory + flash on a small board designed to plug into a carrier via board-to-board mating connectors. Some SoMs include on-board debug peripherals (Ethernet, microSD, USB-UART) that are usable without a carrier.

memory.title: Memory
memory.description: On-SoM DRAM. List one entry per distinct memory bank.

eeprom.title: EEPROM
eeprom.description: On-SoM EEPROMs (typically MAC address, unique board ID). One entry per chip. Useful regardless of carrier — flattens into the kit at build time.

analog.title: Analog
analog.description: Discrete ADCs, DACs, RF transceivers, and AFEs soldered to the SoM.

m2.title: M.2
m2.description: M.2 sockets on the SoM (rare, but some SoMs include them for SSD storage or WiFi).

usb.title: USB
usb.description: USB interfaces accessible to the FPGA design.

carrier.title: SoM Carrier
carrier.description: A carrier board that mates with one or more SoMs to provide user I/O. Has no FPGA of its own — the FPGA comes from the SoM. Compatibility with SoMs is recorded as som-mates relationships, not in the carrier file.

flash.title: Flash
flash.description: On-carrier non-volatile flash. Less common than SoM-side flash (the SoM usually owns configuration + Linux rootfs flash), but some carriers carry their own on-board eMMC or QSPI for application data. One entry per chip. Flattened kits get the union of SoM-side and carrier-side flash.

eeprom.title: EEPROM
eeprom.description: On-carrier EEPROMs (rare — the SoM typically owns the identity EEPROM, but some carriers have their own user-data EEPROM).

analog.title: Analog
analog.description: Discrete ADCs, DACs, RF transceivers, and AFEs soldered to the carrier.

kit.title: Kit (SoM + Carrier)
kit.description: A bundled product comprising a SoM and a carrier (and possibly extras). All feature data is inherited from the composed entities at build time — kit files only contain identity, price, composition, and optional extras.

composition.title: Composition
composition.description: MPN references to the SoM and carrier that compose this kit. Both must exist as entities in soms/ and carriers/. The build script flattens the kit by merging SoM + carrier features into a single resolved record.

som.title: SoM MPN
som.description: MPN of the SoM in soms/<vendor>/<mpn>.json.

carrier.title: Carrier MPN
carrier.description: MPN of the carrier in carriers/<vendor>/<mpn>.json.

extras.title: Extras
extras.description: Additional items bundled with the kit beyond the SoM and carrier (cables, PSUs, antennas, etc.).

fmc_card.title: FMC Card
fmc_card.description: An FPGA Mezzanine Card (FMC LPC / HPC / FMC+) that plugs into an FMC site on a board or carrier. Compatibility is recorded as fmc-mates relationships, not in the card file. vadj range will live here once we model it — schema reserves the spot.

connector_type.title: FMC Connector Type
connector_type.description: LPC (160-pin VITA 57.1), HPC (400-pin VITA 57.1), or FMC+ (560-pin VITA 57.4).

vadj_min.title: VADJ Min (V)
vadj_min.description: Minimum VADJ supply voltage the card accepts. For fixed-voltage cards (e.g. 1.8V-only variants), set vadj_min == vadj_max.

vadj_max.title: VADJ Max (V)
vadj_max.description: Maximum VADJ supply voltage the card accepts.

eeprom.title: EEPROM
eeprom.description: On-card EEPROMs (VITA 57.1 IPMI EEPROM holds board ID and is common on FMC cards).

analog.title: Analog
analog.description: Discrete ADCs, DACs, RF transceivers, and AFEs on the FMC card.

som_mates.title: SoM Compatibility Bundle
som_mates.description: One file per carrier listing all SoMs that physically and electrically mate with it. Filename: relationships/som-mates/<carrier-vendor>/<carrier-mpn>.json — the parent folder matches the carrier's vendor (mirrors carriers/<vendor>/<mpn>.json), and the filename stem must match the top-level `carrier` field.

carrier.title: Carrier MPN
carrier.description: MPN of the carrier in carriers/<vendor>/<mpn>.json. Must match the filename.

som.title: SoM MPN
som.description: MPN of the SoM in soms/<vendor>/<mpn>.json.

fmc_mates.title: FMC Card Compatibility Bundle
fmc_mates.description: One file per FMC card listing all hosts (standalone boards, kits, or carriers) it mates with. Filename: relationships/fmc-mates/<fmc-card-vendor>/<fmc-card-mpn>.json — the parent folder matches the card's vendor (mirrors fmc-cards/<vendor>/<mpn>.json), and the filename stem must match the top-level `fmc_card` field.

fmc_card.title: FMC Card MPN
fmc_card.description: MPN of the FMC card in fmc-cards/<vendor>/<mpn>.json. Must match the filename.

host.title: Host MPN
host.description: MPN of the host (standalone board, kit, or carrier).

host_type.title: Host Type
host_type.description: Which entity folder the host lives in. Drives where the build script looks for the host file.

target_slot.title: Target Slot
target_slot.description: Name of the specific FMC slot on the host (matches a slot name in the host's fmc[] array). Omit if the relationship applies to any slot on the host. One entry per slot, so a host with multiple compatible sites gets multiple entries.

mpn.title: Part Number
mpn.description: Manufacturer part number (MPN / SKU). Must be unique within its entity type. URL-safe characters only (letters, digits, hyphens, dots, underscores). For non-buyable carriers (e.g. the KV260's carrier, which is only sold inside the kit), invent a stable MPN like <kit-mpn>-Carrier.

url.title: Product Page
url.description: Product page URL. For carriers that are not sold standalone, point at the parent kit's URL.

vendor.title: Vendor
vendor.description: Vendor key (must match a key in vendors.json board_vendors).

price_or_null.title: Price
price_or_null.description: Price. Use null when the price is unknown, not published, or the entity is not sold standalone.

verified_by.title: Verified By
verified_by.description: Who asserts this compatibility. 'vendor' = officially supported by the vendor. 'user' = community / user-reported. A future 'fpgadeveloper' value will indicate automated verification from vadj range + pin assignment data.

relationship_notes.title: Notes
relationship_notes.description: Free-text caveats specific to this combination (e.g. 'Only port 0 is usable on HPC1', 'Requires Vivado 2022.1+', 'Needs cooling modification').

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP).

vendor.description: Silicon vendor key (must match a key in vendors.json silicon_vendors).

type.description: Physical form factor of the PCIe interface. M.2 sockets and M.2 form-factor boards live in a separate `m2` array, not here — M.2 is a multi-protocol form factor (PCIe / SATA / USB), so modelling it under `pcie` was misleading. MCIO is the SFF-TA-1016 cabled PCIe connector (Mini Cool Edge IO). MiniPCIe slots are PCIe x1 + USB + (optionally) mSATA — use `msata_capable: true` to mark slots that also accept mSATA SSDs.

msata_capable.description: Set on MiniPCIe slots that also accept mSATA SSDs (the slot is electrically multiplexed). Not a separate storage entry — the physical socket is one connector.

m2_interface.description: An M.2 connector — either a socket the board provides, or the M.2 edge-connector of a board that IS an M.2 module. M.2 is a physical form factor, not an interface, so the protocols routed to the connector are listed separately in `interfaces`.

key.description: M.2 keying. M-key = PCIe x4 NVMe (the common storage slot), E-key = WiFi/BT modules (PCIe x1 + USB + SDIO), B-key = WWAN / older SATA SSDs, B+M-key = double-keyed card / socket compatible with both.

direction.description: socket = the board PROVIDES an M.2 receptacle (you plug a module into it — e.g. Alinx AXU5EVB-P). form-factor = the BOARD itself is in M.2 form factor and its edge connector plugs into a host (e.g. RHS Research LiteFury / NiteFury).

interfaces.description: Protocols actually routed to this M.2 connector. Most M-key sockets carry PCIe (NVMe); the Xilinx ZCU series carry SATA only; E-key sockets typically carry PCIe + USB. When omitted, defaults are inferred by key (M-key/B+M-key → PCIe, E-key → PCIe+USB, B-key → USB).

lanes.description: Number of PCIe lanes routed (only meaningful when `interfaces` includes PCIe).

gen.description: PCIe generation (1-6). Only meaningful when `interfaces` includes PCIe.

length.description: Module length in M.2 nomenclature (WWLL, where WW=width=22mm, LL=length in mm). 2280 is the dominant size for NVMe SSDs; 2230 is common for WiFi modules.

ports.description: Number of ports at this speed.

usb_connector.description: Physical USB connector on the board.

speed.description: USB version (e.g. 2.0 = High-Speed 480 Mbps, 3.0 = SuperSpeed 5 Gbps).

role.description: host = peripherals plug in (Type-A), device = board acts as peripheral, otg = either.

ports.description: Number of physical ports of this kind. Defaults to 1 if omitted.

functions.description: What the bridge does — typically ["jtag", "uart"] for combined chips like FT2232. 'i2c' covers bridge chips that also expose an I2C utility channel (common on Lattice FTDI-based eval boards).

ports.description: Number of physical ports of this kind. Defaults to 1 if omitted.

type.description: Flash family. QSPI/OSPI/SPI/BPI are NOR (typically used for FPGA configuration + some user data). eMMC is managed NAND for Linux rootfs storage. NAND is raw NAND.

size_mb.description: Total capacity of this chip in megabytes. 1 GB → 1024.

width_bits.description: Data bus width. Usually implicit from type (SPI=1, QSPI=4, OSPI=8). Useful to set for BPI (typically 16) and NAND (8 or 16).

type.description: DRAM family. 'SDRAM' is single-data-rate SDRAM; 'LPDDR' is the original low-power/mobile DDR (LPDDR1). HBM variants are on-package, not strictly DDR — listed here because they fill the same role in spec sheets. RLD3 (reduced-latency DRAM), HyperRAM (serial/octal DRAM), and PSRAM (pseudo-static RAM) are specialty DRAM types found on smaller or high-bandwidth boards.

size_mb.description: Total capacity of this bank in megabytes. 4 GB → 4096.

form_factor.description: Physical form. 'component' = soldered DRAM (default). Slot-based = sodimm / dimm / etc.

width_bits.description: Data bus width. 72 typically implies ECC (64-bit data + 8-bit ECC); 40 and 80 are likewise data+ECC widths on some SoMs. 8 is a narrow-bus single DDR3 chip. 48 is a non-ECC bank built from three 16-bit chips. 18/36 are common for QDR-II+ / QDR-IV high-bandwidth SRAM.

ecc.description: True when this bank has ECC (error-correcting) support.

video.title: Video I/O
video.description: Video input/output interfaces. Only include keys with non-zero values.

networking.title: Networking
networking.description: All networking interfaces — Ethernet ports plus high-speed optical/copper cages.

ethernet.title: RJ45
ethernet.description: Wired Ethernet ports (RJ45 connectors), grouped by link speed. Separate from optical/copper SFP/QSFP cages which sit alongside this in the same `networking` object.

qsfp.title: QSFP
qsfp.description: QSFP cage, 4x1G — 4 GbE (rare on FPGA boards).

fmc_slot.title: FMC Slot
fmc_slot.description: A single FMC site on a host. vadj_min/vadj_max are reserved for future automated compatibility checking.

slot.title: Slot Name
slot.description: Name the vendor prints on the silkscreen (e.g. HPC0, HPC1, LPC, J3). Used by fmc-mates relationships to target a specific slot.

vadj_min.title: VADJ Min (V)
vadj_min.description: Minimum VADJ voltage supported by this slot. Reserved for future fpgadeveloper-validated compatibility.

vadj_max.title: VADJ Max (V)
vadj_max.description: Maximum VADJ voltage supported by this slot.

expansion.title: Expansion
expansion.description: Expansion connectors and headers. RFMC is kept as a count for now — it will be promoted to a richer first-class form once we model RFMC mezzanine cards.

fmc.title: FMC
fmc.description: FMC sites on the board/carrier/kit. One entry per slot. Each slot is a {slot, type} object — slot names appear on the silkscreen and are referenced by fmc-mates relationships.

gpio_header.title: GPIO Header
gpio_header.description: Count of generic GPIO breakout headers (no standardised pinout — pin count varies).

xadc_header.title: XADC Header
xadc_header.description: Count of XADC / SYSMON / SmartVIO analog-channel breakout headers (common on Xilinx 7-series and Ultrascale eval boards).

analog_chip.title: Analog Chip
analog_chip.description: A discrete ADC, DAC, RF transceiver, or analog front-end soldered to the board — distinct from the FPGA's built-in XADC/SYSMON, and from chips on FMC/expansion daughter cards.

role.title: Role
role.description: Chip's function. adc/dac = standalone sample-rate-and-bits chip; rf_transceiver = wideband RF AFE (AD9361 etc.); afe = generic analog conditioning frontend.

part.title: Part Number
part.description: Manufacturer part number (e.g. LTC2387, AD9361). Optional but recommended — lets users search by chip.

channels.title: Channels
channels.description: Number of channels on this chip.

frequency_min_mhz.title: Min Frequency (MHz)
frequency_min_mhz.description: Minimum RF operating frequency. RF transceiver / AFE only.

frequency_max_mhz.title: Max Frequency (MHz)
frequency_max_mhz.description: Maximum RF operating frequency. RF transceiver / AFE only.

sensor.title: Sensor
sensor.description: An on-board sensor chip. One entry per sensor.

type.title: Sensor Type
type.description: Sensor category. 'imu' implies combined accelerometer + gyroscope (and usually magnetometer). 'camera' is an image sensor soldered to the board — distinct from video.mipi_csi, which counts camera connectors.

part.title: Part Number
part.description: Manufacturer part number (e.g. MPU-9250, TMP102). Optional but recommended.

audio.title: Audio
audio.description: On-board audio I/O. Distinct from analog[] — captures user-facing audio jacks and the codec routing them.

codec.title: Codec Part
codec.description: Audio codec chip part number (e.g. SSM2603, WM8731, TLV320AIC23).

clocking.title: Clocking
clocking.description: Reference-clock features beyond the FPGA's own internal oscillators.

programmable.title: Programmable Clock
programmable.description: Board includes a programmable clock generator (Si5xxx, IDT FemtoClock, etc.).

sma_clock_in.title: Clock Input SMA
sma_clock_in.description: Count of SMA connectors for external reference-clock inputs.

gps_disciplined.title: GPS-Disciplined Oscillator
gps_disciplined.description: Board has an on-board GPSDO for time-locked operation.

high_speed_io.title: High-Speed I/O
high_speed_io.description: Raw transceiver fan-outs and high-speed cabled connectors that expose the FPGA's GTX/GTH/GTY lanes without the PCIe protocol wrapper.

sma_transceiver_pairs.title: SMA Transceiver Pairs
sma_transceiver_pairs.description: Count of SMA pairs (TX+RX) routed to FPGA transceivers.

firefly.title: Samtec FireFly
firefly.description: Count of Samtec FireFly module slots.

slimsas.title: SlimSAS
slimsas.description: Count of SlimSAS cabled connectors (typically PCIe over cable).

displayport_transceiver.title: Mini-DP Transceiver
displayport_transceiver.description: Count of mini-DisplayPort connectors wired to FPGA transceivers (GTP/GTH lanes), not video — a low-cost way to break out MGT lanes. Seen on Numato boards.

serial.title: Serial
serial.description: Industrial / automotive serial interfaces with on-board transceivers. The USB-bridged UART/JTAG ports live in usb_bridge, not here.

can.title: CAN
can.description: Count of CAN 2.0 / CAN-FD transceiver ports.

user_io.title: User I/O
user_io.description: On-board user I/O elements. Counts only — for boot-mode DIP switches and other config-only inputs, use board notes.

leds.title: LEDs
leds.description: Count of user-controllable single-colour LEDs (excludes power/status LEDs and excludes RGB LEDs — those go in rgb_leds).

rgb_leds.title: RGB LEDs
rgb_leds.description: Count of user-controllable RGB / multi-colour LEDs. Counted separately from plain leds because they take 3 FPGA pins each.

dip_switches.title: DIP Switches
dip_switches.description: Count of user DIP switch positions (excludes boot-mode switches).

features.title: Features
features.description: Boolean capability flags that don't warrant their own structured section.

programmable_vadj.title: Programmable VADJ
programmable_vadj.description: User can adjust VADJ / core voltage rails (typically via I2C / PMBus).

battery_backed_rtc.title: Battery-Backed RTC
battery_backed_rtc.description: RTC backed by coin cell or supercap.

secure_element.title: Secure Element
secure_element.description: Discrete TPM / ATECC / equivalent cryptographic chip.

display.title: Display
display.description: Small on-board display panels (character LCD, OLED). Distinct from `video` — video covers source/sink ports to external screens (HDMI, DisplayPort, VGA), `display` covers indicator-class panels soldered to the board.

oled.title: OLED
oled.description: Monochrome / RGB OLED panel resolution as 'WIDTHxHEIGHT' pixels (e.g. '128x32', '128x64', '64x48').

eeprom_chip.title: EEPROM
eeprom_chip.description: A small on-board EEPROM (typically I2C/SPI, used for MAC address, board UID, board identification, or user configuration). One entry per chip.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.description: What this EEPROM stores. SoMs typically combine `mac_address` + `unique_id`. FMC cards typically use `board_id` (VITA 57.1 IPMI EEPROM). `user_data` is for general-purpose EEPROMs available to the FPGA design.

size_kbits.title: Size (Kbits)
size_kbits.description: Capacity in kilobits (1 Kb = 128 bytes). Common values: 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64.

part.title: Part Number
part.description: Manufacturer part number (e.g. 24LC04, AT24C512, M24C32). Optional.
